sentences.base_language: Rim bought her train ticket weeks in advance, excited about visiting her family in another city.
sentences.target_language: اشترت ريم تذكرة القطار قبل أسابيع، متحمِّسةً لزيارة عائلتها في مدينةٍ أخرى.
sentences.romanized: Ishtarat Rīmu tadhkarata l-qiṭāri qabla asābīʿ, mutaḥammisat li-ziyārati ʿāʾilatihā fī madīnatin ukhrā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**اشترت** (Form VIII of شرى) means she bought; Form VIII (افتعل) of this root adds a reflexive nuance — to acquire for oneself — and ت marks the feminine past tense.
**متحمِّسةً** is a Form V active participle (متفعِّل) functioning as a ḥāl, describing Rim's state while she performed the action — she bought the ticket while being excited.

sentences.base_language: On the morning of departure, she arrived at the station with a large suitcase and mixed feelings of excitement and longing.
sentences.target_language: في صباح يوم السفر، وصلت إلى المحطة وبصحبتها حقيبةٌ كبيرةٌ ومشاعر متباينةٌ من الإثارة والشوق.
sentences.romanized: Fī ṣabāḥi yawmi s-safari, waṣalat ilā l-maḥaṭṭati wa-bi-ṣuḥbatihā ḥaqībatun kabīratun wa-mashāʿiru mutabāyinatun min al-ithārati wa-sh-shawq.
sentences.grammarNote:
**وبصحبتها** is a ḥāl clause (بـ + masdar مضاف + pronoun) meaning with her accompanying [it] — describing what she had with her as she arrived; this prepositional ḥāl is common in MSA narrative.
**مشاعر** is the broken plural of مشعر/مشاعرة (feeling/sense); here it follows the pattern مفاعل, one of the high-frequency broken plural patterns for مفعَل-root nouns.

sentences.base_language: The platform was crowded with travelers of all kinds hurrying toward their carriages.
sentences.target_language: كان الرصيف مكتظاً بالمسافرين من شتى الأنواع يتدافعون نحو عرباتهم.
sentences.romanized: Kāna r-raṣīfu muktaẓẓan bil-musāfirīna min shattā l-anwāʿi yatadāfaʿūna naḥwa ʿarabātihim.
sentences.grammarNote:
**كان مكتظاً** is a كان + خبر construction placing the scene in the past; مكتظاً (Form VIII passive participle of كظّ, to be packed) is in the accusative as the predicate of كان.
**يتدافعون** (Form VI of دفع) means to jostle or push one another; Form VI (تفاعل) expresses reciprocal action among a group — each pushing the other — functioning here as a ḥāl clause.

sentences.base_language: She found her reserved window seat and settled in with a novel and some snacks.
sentences.target_language: وجدت مقعدها المحجوز بجانب النافذة واستقرَّت فيه ومعها روايةٌ وبعض الوجبات الخفيفة.
sentences.romanized: Wajdat maqʿadahā l-maḥjūza bi-jānibi n-nāfidhati wa-staqarrat fīhi wa-maʿahā riwāyatun wa-baʿḍu l-wajbāti l-khafīfa.
sentences.grammarNote:
**المحجوز** is a passive participle (Form I مفعول pattern) of حجز (to reserve/book), functioning as an adjective modifying مقعد — the reserved seat.
**استقرَّت** (Form X of قرّ) means she settled/became established; Form X (استفعل) expresses seeking or finding a state — she sought and found stability in the seat.

sentences.base_language: As the train left the city, the buildings gradually gave way to open fields and rolling hills.
sentences.target_language: حين غادر القطار المدينة، أفسحت المباني تدريجياً المجالَ لحقولٍ مفتوحةٍ وتلالٍ متموِّجة.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna ghādara l-qiṭāru l-madīnata, afsaḥat al-mabānī tadrījiyyan al-majāla li-ḥuqūlin maftūḥatin wa-tilālin mutamawwija.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أفسحت المجال لـ** is a set MSA idiom from Form IV of سَحَ (أفسح) meaning to make way for / give room to; Form IV is causative — to cause spaciousness.
**تلال** is the broken plural of تلّة (hill); the pattern فِعَال is a common broken plural for monosyllabic roots with geminate consonants like تلّ.

sentences.base_language: Rim felt a wave of calm settle over her — something about train travel always had that effect on her.
sentences.target_language: أحسَّت ريم بموجةٍ من الهدوء تغمرها — إذ كان للسفر بالقطار دائماً هذا الأثر عليها.
sentences.romanized: Aḥassat Rīmu bi-mawjatin min al-hudūʾi taghmuruha — idh kāna lis-safari bil-qiṭāri dāʾiman hādhā l-atharu ʿalayhā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أحسَّ بـ** (Form IV of حسّ) means to feel/sense something; the obligatory preposition بـ following أحسَّ marks the thing felt — she sensed a wave of calm.
**كان للسفر هذا الأثر** uses كان with a reversed nominal sentence as its predicate; the لـ + noun structure expresses possession — train travel had this effect (lit. this effect was for train travel).

sentences.base_language: After about an hour, the woman sitting across from her began a friendly conversation.
sentences.target_language: بعد نحو ساعةٍ، بادرت المرأة الجالسة في مواجهتها بحديثٍ ودّيٍّ.
sentences.romanized: Baʿda naḥwi sāʿatin, bādarat al-marʾatu l-jālisatu fī muwājahatiha bi-ḥadīthin waddiyy.
sentences.grammarNote:
**بادرت بـ** (Form III of بدر) means to take the initiative with / begin promptly; Form III (فاعل) expresses a directed or reciprocally oriented action — she directed her initiative toward Rim.
**الجالسة** is a Form I active participle (فاعلة) of جلس used as an attributive adjective — the sitting woman; active participles regularly serve as adjectives in Arabic.

sentences.base_language: She turned out to be a retired teacher who took this train every month to visit her grandchildren.
sentences.target_language: تبيَّن أنها مدرِّسةٌ متقاعدةٌ تأخذ هذا القطار كل شهرٍ لتزور أحفادها.
sentences.romanized: Tabayyana annaha mudarrisatun mutaqāʿidatun taʾkhudhu hādhā l-qiṭāra kulla shahrin li-tazūra aḥfādahā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تبيَّن** (Form V of بان) means it became clear / it turned out; Form V (تفعَّل) is the reflexive of Form II — where بيَّن means to clarify, تبيَّن means to become clarified.
**أحفاد** is the broken plural of حفيد (grandchild); the pattern أفعال is among the most productive broken plural patterns for three-consonant roots.

sentences.base_language: Rim's stomach growled and she realized she had not eaten anything since early morning.
sentences.target_language: قرقر معدة ريم فأدركت أنها لم تأكل شيئاً منذ الصباح الباكر.
sentences.romanized: Qarqara miʿdatu Rīma fa-adrakata annaha lam taʾkul shayʾan mundhu ṣ-ṣabāḥi l-bākir.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أدركت** (Form IV of درك) means she realized/perceived; Form IV (أفعل) is regularly causative or ingressive — to reach or arrive at understanding.
**لم تأكل** uses لم + مضارع مجزوم (jussive) to negate a past action absolutely; the jussive form تأكل (shortened from تأكلُ) is the standard negation of a completed past event.

sentences.base_language: She walked to the dining car and ordered a warm meal and a cup of tea.
sentences.target_language: مشت إلى عربة المطعم وطلبت وجبةً دافئةً وفنجاناً من الشاي.
sentences.romanized: Mashat ilā ʿarabati l-maṭʿami wa-ṭalabat wajbatan dāfiʾatan wa-finjānan min ash-shāy.
sentences.grammarNote:
**عربة المطعم** is an إضافة (genitive construct) meaning the carriage of the restaurant / the dining car; the مضاف (عربة) is made definite by the مضاف إليه (المطعم) without needing its own definite article.
**طلبت** (Form I of طلب) means she ordered/requested; the فَعَلَت pattern is the regular feminine past tense Form I, and طلب is one of the core verbs for requesting in MSA.

sentences.base_language: The simple food tasted better than expected, perhaps because of the scenery rushing past outside.
sentences.target_language: بدا الطعام البسيط ألذَّ مما كان متوقَّعاً، ربما بسبب المشاهد الخارجية التي كانت تتساقط أمامها بسرعةٍ.
sentences.romanized: Badā ṭ-ṭaʿāmu l-basīṭu aladdha mimmā kāna mutawqqaʿan, rubbamā bi-sababi l-mashāhidi l-khārijiyyati l-latī kānat tatasāqaṭu amāmahā bi-surʿa.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ألذَّ** is the comparative/superlative of لذيذ (delicious), following the أفعل elative pattern; comparatives of adjectives with more than three root consonants use أكثر + masdar, but trilateral adjectives form the elative directly.
**كانت تتساقط** is كان + مضارع expressing past progressive — the scenes were rushing past (continuously); كان + imperfect describes an ongoing background action in past narrative.

sentences.base_language: Back at her seat, she dozed off briefly and woke up just as the train passed through a long tunnel.
sentences.target_language: عادت إلى مقعدها فأغفت قليلاً واستيقظت في اللحظة التي كان القطار يعبر فيها نفقاً طويلاً.
sentences.romanized: ʿĀdat ilā maqʿadihā fa-aghfat qalīlan wa-stayqaẓat fī l-laḥẓati l-latī kāna l-qiṭāru yaʿburu fīhā nafaqan ṭawīlā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أغفت** (Form IV of غَفَا) means she dozed off; Form IV is ingressive here — to enter into a state of dozing — a common use for Form IV with stative roots.
**في اللحظة التي كان يعبر فيها** is a relative clause with the resumptive adverb فيها; when the relative pronoun serves as an adverbial object, a resumptive pronoun in the clause is obligatory — the moment in which the train was passing.

sentences.base_language: The conductor came to check tickets and announced that the next stop would be in twenty minutes.
sentences.target_language: جاء المراقب ليفحص التذاكر وأعلن أنَّ المحطة القادمة ستكون بعد عشرين دقيقة.
sentences.romanized: Jāʾa l-murāqibu li-yafḥaṣa t-tadhākira wa-aʿlana anna l-maḥaṭṭata l-qādimata sa-takūnu baʿda ʿishrīna daqīqa.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ليفحص** is لـ + مضارع منصوب (subjunctive) expressing purpose — in order to check; the لام التعليل with a subjunctive verb is the standard way to express purpose after motion verbs in MSA.
**أنَّ** takes its subject المحطةَ القادمةَ in the accusative; both the noun and its adjective carry accusative fatḥa endings, demonstrating full case concord in the أنَّ clause.

sentences.base_language: Rim's back ached from sitting so long and she stood up to stretch her legs in the corridor.
sentences.target_language: ألَمَ ظهر ريم من طول الجلوس فنهضت لتمدَّ ساقيها في الممر.
sentences.romanized: Alima ẓahru Rīma min ṭūli l-julūsi fa-nahaḍat li-tamadd sāqayhā fī l-mamarr.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ألَمَ** (Form I of ألم) is an intransitive verb meaning to ache/hurt; the subject ظهر ريم (Rim's back) is an إضافة functioning as the sentence subject — Arabic verbal sentences place the verb first.
**ساقيها** is the dual accusative of ساق (leg) with the possessive pronoun ـها; the dual oblique form سَاقَيْن loses its ن in إضافة and before a possessive pronoun — ساقَيها = her two legs.

sentences.base_language: A small child in the seat ahead was pointing excitedly at horses through the window and laughing.
sentences.target_language: كان طفلٌ صغيرٌ في المقعد الأمامي يشير بحماسٍ إلى الخيول من النافذة ويضحك.
sentences.romanized: Kāna ṭiflun ṣaghīrun fī l-maqʿadi l-amāmiyyi yushīru bi-ḥamāsin ilā l-khuyūli min an-nāfidhati wa-yaḍḥak.
sentences.grammarNote:
**كان يشير ويضحك** is كان + مضارع (imperfect) expressing past progressive — the child was pointing and laughing (continuously); the two coordinated imperfect verbs share a single كان, describing simultaneous ongoing actions.
**الخيول** is the broken plural of خيل/حصان; خيول follows the pattern فعول, one of the broken plural patterns for animals — parallel to بيوت (houses, pl. of بيت).

sentences.base_language: Rim smiled and thought about her own childhood journeys by train with her parents.
sentences.target_language: ابتسمت ريم وتذكَّرت رحلاتها هي نفسها بالقطار في طفولتها برفقة والديها.
sentences.romanized: Ibtasamat Rīmu wa-tadhakkaarat riḥlatahā hiya nafsahā bil-qiṭāri fī ṭufūlatihā bi-rifqati wālidayhā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ابتسمت** (Form VIII of بسم) means she smiled; Form VIII (افتعل) adds a reflexive or ingressive sense — to enter into a smile — distinguishing it from the simple Form I.
**والديها** is the dual genitive of والد (parent/father) with possessive ـها; the dual oblique form وَالِدَيْن loses its ن in إضافة — والِدَيْها = her two parents.

sentences.base_language: As the train began to slow on approach to the final station, she started gathering her belongings with growing excitement.
sentences.target_language: حين بدأ القطار يتباطأ اقترابًا من المحطة الأخيرة، شرعت تجمع أغراضها بإثارةٍ متصاعدة.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna badaʾa l-qiṭāru yatabāṭaʾu qtirāban min al-maḥaṭṭati l-akhīra, sharaʿat tajmaʿu aghrāḍahā bi-ithāratin mutaṣāʿida.
sentences.grammarNote:
**يتباطأ** (Form V of بطؤ) means to slow down gradually; Form V (تفعَّل) of this root expresses a gradual or deliberate entry into a state — becoming slow by degrees.
**شرعت تجمع** is شرع + مضارع, a periphrastic construction meaning she began / started to gather; شرع (to begin/start) takes a bare imperfect without أن, a feature distinguishing it from other inchoative verbs.

sentences.base_language: She could already picture her mother and younger brother standing on the platform waiting for her.
sentences.target_language: كانت تتخيَّل بالفعل أمَّها وأخاها الأصغر واقفَيْن على الرصيف ينتظرانها.
sentences.romanized: Kānat tatakhayyal bil-fiʿli ummahā wa-akhāhā l-aṣghara wāqifayni ʿalā r-raṣīfi yantaẓirānhā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تتخيَّل** (Form V of خَيَّل) means to imagine/picture; Form V (تفعَّل) is reflexive of Form II — where خيَّل means to make something appear, تخيَّل means to make an image appear to oneself.
**واقفَيْن ينتظرانها** shows dual agreement: واقفَيْن (dual accusative ḥāl participle) and ينتظرانها (dual imperfect verb with dual suffix ـان + object ـها) both agree with the dual subject أمّها وأخاها.

sentences.base_language: The train arrived exactly on time and Rim stepped onto the platform feeling stiff but very happy.
sentences.target_language: وصل القطار في موعده تماماً ونزلت ريم إلى الرصيف تحس بتصلُّبٍ في جسدها لكنها سعيدةٌ جداً.
sentences.romanized: Waṣala l-qiṭāru fī mawʿidihī tamāman wa-nazalat Rīmu ilā r-raṣīfi tuḥissu bi-taṣallub fī jasadihā lākinnhā saʿīdatun jiddan.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تحس بتصلُّب** is أحسَّ بـ in the present tense functioning as a ḥāl — she stepped down while feeling stiffness; تصلُّب is the Form V masdar (تفعُّل pattern) of صَلَّب meaning the act of becoming stiff.
**لكنَّها سعيدةٌ** uses لكنَّ (the strong form of but) which takes its subject in the accusative — لكنَّ + accusative pronoun ـها; the predicate سعيدةٌ is nominative as the خبر.

sentences.base_language: Walking toward the exit with her suitcase, she decided that however fast planes were, nothing could replace the pleasure of traveling by train.
sentences.target_language: وهي تسير نحو المخرج بحقيبتها، قرَّرت أنَّه مهما كانت الطائرات سريعةً، لا شيء يمكن أن يحلَّ محلَّ متعة السفر بالقطار.
sentences.romanized: Wa-hiya tasīru naḥwa l-makhraji bi-ḥaqībatihā, qarrarat annahu mahmā kānat aṭ-ṭāʾirātu sarīʿatan, lā shayʾa yumkinu an yaḥulla maḥalla mutʿati s-safari bil-qiṭār.
sentences.grammarNote:
**مهما كانت ... سريعةً** uses مهما (however much/no matter how) as a concessive particle; it triggers كان with its predicate in the accusative — however fast the planes might be — expressing concession before the main assertion.
**يحلَّ محلَّ** (Form I of حلّ) is the fixed MSA idiom meaning to replace / take the place of; محلَّ is the accusative مفعول به — to occupy the place of something — and متعة السفر is a chained إضافة.
